claim_ladder_note: Per docs/benchmarks/claim-ladder.md L1: functional correctness (workload runs to completion, outputs match a golden reference) is supportable; this evidence supports ISA functional correctness only. It does NOT support wall-clock/phone-class performance, silicon power, or Android/CTS claims.
flow: CVA6 supported regression flow (verif/sim/cva6.py); per-test tandem run of the Verilator RTL model (veri-testharness) and the Spike golden ISS, with RVFI trace-CSV comparison. Same evidence class as core-v-verif.

model.isolation: Verilated into a private symlink-farm tree (build/isa-conformance/cva6-iso) with its own work-ver, so the shared external/cva6/cva6/work-ver model held by the benchmark agent was never built into or clobbered.

skipped_tests.reason: upstream testlist iterations:0 (tdata1 read differs spike 0x2000000000000000 vs cva6 0x0)
upstream testlist iterations:0 (cva6 does not record illegal instructions in trace log)
upstream testlist iterations:0 (error with spike reference)

compare_records.note: cva6.py iss_regr.log summary: '193 PASSED, 0 FAILED' (193 RVFI compare records over 192 distinct directed tests; one test name compared twice).

suites.description: RISC-V architectural compliance suite (riscv-non-isa/riscv-arch-test), Spike as reference signature model; per-test tandem RVFI compare of the CVA6 Verilator RTL model against Spike.
suites.name: riscv-arch-test
suites.toolchain_resolution: The prior blocker -- xPack/GNU binutils rejecting the riscv-arch-test TEST_JALR_OP macro's `la x0,<sym>` pseudo-instruction -- is resolved by routing the suite's compile+link through an LLVM clang shim (scripts/run_e1_isa_conformance.sh: riscv-arch-clang, clang -target riscv64-unknown-elf, GNU ld via -fuse-ld, -Wl,-melf64lriscv). clang's integrated assembler accepts `la x0`. Object code is functionally identical RV64GC and the same ELF runs on both the RTL model and Spike, so the assembler choice cannot bias the signature comparison.

suites.blocked_reason: Not run. The riscv-compliance testlist (testlist_riscv-compliance-cv64a6_imafdc_sv39.yaml) is vendored, but the legacy compliance flow shares the same riscv-arch-test source tree and toolchain, so it inherits the same xPack-binutils `la x0` assembler incompatibility blocking riscv-arch-test.
suites.name: riscv-compliance
suites.remediation: Resolve the riscv-arch-test toolchain blocker above, then add a riscv-compliance branch to scripts/run_e1_isa_conformance.sh using verif/regress/dv-riscv-compliance.sh as the reference.
suites.toolchain_note: The shared `la x0` assembler blocker is resolved via the same LLVM clang shim (use_arch_test_cc) now proven on riscv-arch-test. riscv-compliance remains not-yet-run pending a vendored testlist_riscv-compliance-cv64a6_imafdc_sv39.yaml and the legacy riscv-test-env source tree.

verdict: The cv64a6_imafdc_sv39 RTL model (== E1 e1-pro little core) passes 416/416 executed CVA6 ISA tests with 0 failures, each verified by RVFI trace comparison against the Spike golden ISS: 118 machine-mode + 106 Sv39 virtual-mode riscv-tests, plus 192 riscv-arch-test architectural-compliance directed tests (cva6.py iss_regr.log: '193 PASSED, 0 FAILED'). The riscv-arch-test suite -- previously fail-closed BLOCKED at 15/221 on a host-binutils `la x0` assembler incompatibility -- now runs end to end via an LLVM clang compile shim, with every executed test matching Spike. This is measured ISA-correctness evidence of the same class core-v-verif provides, valid at L1_RTL_FULL_SOC. riscv-compliance remains not-yet-run (testlist + legacy riscv-test-env not vendored); the toolchain blocker it shared with riscv-arch-test is resolved.
